signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike + Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD testing 0.7191 following RBA's rate hike to 4.35%. COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) indicates institutional backing for the move, while retail sentiment is at an 85% short extreme. This institutional vs. retail divergence at a 1-month high suggests a high-conviction trend; retail capitulation risk provides further fuel for upside momentum. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a +60bp carry advantage for AUD, supporting structural bids.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23% long) shows speculators are heavily fading the RBNZ despite a hawkish market bias and a 'Hike' signal for the May 27 meeting. With NZD/USD at 0.5894 and a +150bp carry deficit vs USD, any hawkish delivery or signal from the RBNZ creates significant short-squeeze risk as crowded speculative shorts are forced to unwind. Watch for a break above 0.5950 to confirm the squeeze.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.81 remains supported by a +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%). While Trump-Xi headlines regarding Iran introduce geopolitical noise, JPY safe-haven demand remains marginal with VIX at 17.4 (normal volatility). JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains a crowded carry trade; intervention risk rises significantly if price tests the 160.00 psychological handle.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.731 vs norm of -0.138 (z=+1.86σ) indicates a significant regime break where EUR is tracking equity strength rather than traditional macro drivers. ECB's Villeroy confirms no immediate signs to raise rates (ECB 2% ON HOLD), maintaining a -175bp carry disadvantage vs USD. Price at 1.1704 remains range-bound; the correlation break suggests EUR upside is currently a proxy for European risk-on sentiment rather than rate expectations.
